checks.label: Ubuntu 22.04+
checks.fix: Upgrade to Ubuntu 22.04 LTS: do-release-upgrade

checks.label: CPU ≥ 2 cores
checks.fix: Resize VPS to at least 2 vCPUs

checks.label: RAM ≥ 1GB
checks.fix: Resize VPS to at least 2GB RAM

checks.label: Disk ≥ 5GB free
checks.fix: Free disk space or expand volume

checks.label: Swap configured
checks.fix: sudo fallocate -l 2G /swapfile && sudo chmod 600 /swapfile && sudo mkswap /swapfile && sudo swapon /swapfile

checks.label: Timezone (UTC preferred)
checks.fix: sudo timedatectl set-timezone UTC

checks.label: SSH service running
checks.detail: SSH: Command failed: systemctl is-active ssh 2>/dev/null || systemctl is-active sshd 2>/dev/null
checks.fix: sudo systemctl enable --now ssh

checks.label: UFW firewall active
checks.fix: sudo ufw allow 22 && sudo ufw allow 80 && sudo ufw allow 443 && sudo ufw --force enable

checks.label: Fail2Ban active
checks.detail: Fail2Ban: Command failed: systemctl is-active fail2ban 2>/dev/null
checks.fix: sudo apt install fail2ban -y && sudo systemctl enable --now fail2ban

checks.label: Cron daemon active
checks.detail: Cron: Command failed: systemctl is-active cron 2>/dev/null || systemctl is-active crond 2>/dev/null
checks.fix: sudo systemctl enable --now cron

checks.label: PM2 running (≥1 online process)
checks.fix: pm2 start ecosystem.config.cjs --env production

checks.label: Nginx installed
checks.fix: sudo apt install nginx -y && sudo systemctl enable --now nginx

checks.label: Nginx service running
checks.detail: nginx: Command failed: systemctl is-active nginx 2>/dev/null
checks.fix: sudo systemctl start nginx && sudo systemctl enable nginx

checks.label: Nginx config valid (nginx -t)
checks.fix: Edit /etc/nginx/sites-available/ooplix — fix the reported syntax error and sudo nginx -t

checks.label: Ooplix nginx site config exists
checks.fix: sudo cp nginx.conf /etc/nginx/sites-available/ooplix && sudo ln -s /etc/nginx/sites-available/ooplix /etc/nginx/sites-enabled/ && sudo nginx -t && sudo systemctl reload nginx

checks.label: HTTPS (port 443) listening
checks.fix: Ensure SSL cert is installed and nginx is configured for HTTPS

checks.label: HTTP → HTTPS redirect
checks.fix: Add 'return 301 https://$host$request_uri;' in the :80 server block

checks.label: Gzip compression enabled
checks.fix: Add 'gzip on; gzip_types text/plain text/css application/json application/javascript;' to nginx.conf

checks.label: Static asset caching configured
checks.fix: Add 'expires 1y; add_header Cache-Control "public, immutable";' for /static/ location in nginx.conf

checks.label: Security headers (HSTS/X-Frame/X-Content-Type)
checks.detail: HSTS: false, X-Frame: false, X-Content-Type: false
checks.fix: Add HSTS, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, X-XSS-Protection in nginx.conf server block

checks.label: Large upload (client_max_body_size) set
checks.fix: Add 'client_max_body_size 50m;' to the server block in nginx.conf

checks.label: WebSocket proxying configured
checks.detail: WebSocket headers not found in nginx.conf
checks.fix: Add: proxy_http_version 1.1; proxy_set_header Upgrade $http_upgrade; proxy_set_header Connection 'upgrade';

checks.label: SSL certificate file exists
checks.fix: sudo certbot certonly --nginx -d app.ooplix.com -d www.ooplix.com -d ooplix.com

checks.label: SSL cert valid for ≥ 30 days
checks.fix: sudo certbot renew --force-renewal

checks.label: Certbot auto-renew active
checks.fix: sudo systemctl enable certbot.timer && sudo systemctl start certbot.timer  OR  add: 0 12 * * * /usr/bin/certbot renew --quiet

checks.label: OCSP stapling enabled
checks.fix: Add 'ssl_stapling on; ssl_stapling_verify on;' to nginx.conf HTTPS server block

checks.label: TLS 1.0/1.1 disabled (TLS 1.2+ only)
checks.fix: Set 'ssl_protocols TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3;' in nginx.conf

checks.label: A record for ooplix.com
checks.fix: Add A record: ooplix.com → <VPS_IP> in your DNS provider

checks.label: AAAA record for ooplix.com
checks.fix: Add AAAA record pointing to your VPS IPv6 address

checks.label: www.ooplix.com resolves
checks.fix: Add CNAME: www → ooplix.com OR A record for www

checks.label: MX record for ooplix.com
checks.fix: Add MX records via your email provider (e.g., Google Workspace, Resend)

checks.label: TXT records for ooplix.com
checks.fix: Add domain verification TXT records for your services

checks.label: SPF record
checks.fix: Add TXT: v=spf1 include:_spf.resend.com include:sendgrid.net ~all (match your email provider)

checks.label: DKIM record
checks.detail: No DKIM record at resend._domainkey.ooplix.com: ENOTFOUND
checks.fix: Configure DKIM via your email provider (Resend: resend._domainkey.ooplix.com → provided TXT)

checks.label: DMARC record
checks.fix: Add TXT _dmarc.ooplix.com: v=DMARC1; p=quarantine; rua=mailto:dmarc@ooplix.com

checks.label: CAA record
checks.fix: Add CAA record: 0 issue "letsencrypt.org" for ooplix.com

checks.label: DNS propagated (3/3 resolvers)
checks.fix: DNS propagation takes up to 48h. Verify at whatsmydns.net

checks.label: ooplix.com resolves
checks.fix: Add DNS A/CNAME for ooplix.com → VPS IP

checks.label: ooplix.com HTTPS responds
checks.fix: Ensure nginx SSL config covers this subdomain

checks.label: www.ooplix.com resolves
checks.fix: Add DNS A/CNAME for www.ooplix.com → VPS IP

checks.label: www.ooplix.com HTTPS responds
checks.fix: Ensure nginx SSL config covers this subdomain

checks.label: app.ooplix.com resolves
checks.fix: Add DNS A/CNAME for app.ooplix.com → VPS IP

checks.label: app.ooplix.com HTTPS responds
checks.fix: Ensure nginx SSL config covers this subdomain

checks.label: api.ooplix.com resolves
checks.fix: Add DNS A/CNAME for api.ooplix.com → VPS IP

checks.label: api.ooplix.com HTTPS responds
checks.fix: Ensure nginx SSL config covers this subdomain

checks.label: docs.ooplix.com resolves
checks.fix: Add DNS A/CNAME for docs.ooplix.com → VPS IP

checks.label: status.ooplix.com resolves
checks.fix: Add DNS A/CNAME for status.ooplix.com → VPS IP

checks.label: cdn.ooplix.com resolves
checks.fix: Add DNS A/CNAME for cdn.ooplix.com → VPS IP

checks.label: Frontend build artifacts present
checks.fix: cd frontend && npm ci && npm run build

checks.label: Backend entry point (backend/server.js)
checks.fix: Ensure backend/server.js exists and is the PM2 entry point

checks.label: PM2 ecosystem config (ecosystem.config.cjs)
checks.fix: PM2 ecosystem config required: pm2 start ecosystem.config.cjs --env production

checks.label: PM2 jarvis-os process online
checks.fix: pm2 start ecosystem.config.cjs --env production && pm2 save

checks.label: Health endpoint /api/health responds 200
checks.fix: Ensure backend is running: pm2 restart jarvis-os && pm2 logs jarvis-os --lines 20

checks.label: Electron auto-updater configured
checks.detail: electron-builder publish config or GH_TOKEN found
checks.fix: Set GH_TOKEN and add publish: {provider: github} to package.json build config

checks.label: PM2 restart capability
checks.fix: pm2 start ecosystem.config.cjs --env production && pm2 save && pm2 startup

checks.label: Git rollback capability (git log reachable)
checks.detail: Last commits: fc5c775 feat(pcs-2): Production Credentials Sprint 2 — External Platform Matrix (8 ecosystems, 39 platforms)
checks.fix: Ensure repo is initialized and commits are present for rollback

checks.label: Backup script present (backup.sh / safe-backup.cjs)
checks.fix: Create backup.sh that tarballs data/ and uploads to S3/R2

checks.label: Backups directory exists
checks.fix: mkdir -p backups && chmod 700 backups

checks.label: Backup exists (< 25h old)
checks.fix: Run: ./backup.sh   OR add to cron: 0 2 * * * cd /opt/jarvis-os && ./backup.sh >> logs/backup.log 2>&1

checks.label: data/ directory has files (flat-file DB)
checks.fix: Ensure data/ is included in backup: tar -czf backup.tar.gz data/

checks.label: .env (config) backup awareness
checks.detail: .env file present — ensure it's backed up securely off-site (NOT in git)
checks.fix: Back up .env to a secrets manager (Doppler, AWS Secrets Manager, Vault) — NEVER commit to git

checks.label: Assets (frontend build / uploads) exist
checks.fix: Include frontend/build and uploads/ in your backup script

checks.label: Restore test script present
checks.fix: Create scripts/test-restore.cjs to validate backup integrity on every backup run

checks.label: Latest backup file is readable
checks.fix: Re-run ./backup.sh and verify tar -tzf backups/latest.tar.gz completes without errors

checks.label: Backup retention ≥ 7 copies
checks.fix: Update backup.sh retention: keep last 14 backups with: ls -t backups/*.tar.gz | tail -n +15 | xargs rm -f

checks.label: Off-site backup configured (S3/R2/rsync)
checks.detail: No S3/R2 credentials — backups are local only
checks.fix: Set AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID + AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY (or R2 keys) and upload: aws s3 cp backups/ s3://your-bucket/ --recursive

checks.label: PM2 process monitoring
checks.fix: pm2 start ecosystem.config.cjs --env production && pm2 monit

checks.label: Memory usage < 90%
checks.fix: Investigate memory leak: pm2 logs jarvis-os --lines 50 && pm2 monit

checks.label: CPU usage < 80%
checks.fix: Scale VPS or optimize hot code paths

checks.label: Disk usage < 85%
checks.fix: Clean old logs: pm2 flush && find logs/ -mtime +7 -delete && df -h /

checks.label: Telegram alerts configured
checks.fix: Set TELEGRAM_TOKEN (from @BotFather) and TELEGRAM_OPERATOR_CHAT_ID in .env

checks.label: Log rotation configured
checks.fix: pm2 install pm2-logrotate && pm2 set pm2-logrotate:max_size 50M && pm2 set pm2-logrotate:retain 7

checks.label: Health endpoint accessible
checks.fix: Ensure the backend is running: pm2 restart jarvis-os

checks.label: Security headers (HSTS, X-Frame, X-Content-Type, CSP, Referrer-Policy)
checks.detail: X-Frame: false, X-Content-Type: false, HSTS: false, CSP: false, Referrer: false
checks.fix: Set security headers in nginx.conf: HSTS, X-Frame-Options DENY, X-Content-Type-Options nosniff, CSP

checks.label: JWT_SECRET ≥ 32 characters
checks.fix: Generate: openssl rand -hex 32  then set JWT_SECRET in .env

checks.label: .env excluded from git (.gitignore)
checks.fix: echo '.env' >> .gitignore && git rm --cached .env 2>/dev/null || true

checks.label: .env permissions 600 (owner read/write only)
checks.fix: chmod 600 .env

checks.label: SSH password authentication disabled
checks.detail: Cannot probe sshd config (may be OK in local dev)
checks.fix: Set PasswordAuthentication no in /etc/ssh/sshd_config then: sudo systemctl reload sshd

checks.label: No unexpected ports open
checks.fix: Close unexpected ports: sudo ufw deny

checks.label: Nginx rate limiting configured
checks.fix: Add limit_req_zone and limit_req directives to nginx.conf (see project nginx.conf for reference)

checks.label: 50 users: P95 ≤ 800ms
checks.fix: Optimize hot path: check PM2 logs, nginx proxy timeouts, and in-memory ops

checks.label: 50 users: Error rate ≤ 1%
checks.fix: Check backend logs: pm2 logs jarvis-os --lines 50

checks.label: 50 users: CPU < 90%
checks.fix: Optimize CPU-heavy code paths or scale VPS

checks.label: 50 users: Memory < 1.5GB
checks.fix: Investigate memory usage: pm2 monit && heap profiling

checks.label: 100 users: P95 ≤ 1500ms
checks.fix: Optimize hot path: check PM2 logs, nginx proxy timeouts, and in-memory ops

checks.label: 100 users: Error rate ≤ 2%
checks.fix: Check backend logs: pm2 logs jarvis-os --lines 50

checks.label: 100 users: CPU < 90%
checks.fix: Optimize CPU-heavy code paths or scale VPS

checks.label: 100 users: Memory < 1.5GB
checks.fix: Investigate memory usage: pm2 monit && heap profiling

checks.label: 250 users: P95 ≤ 3000ms
checks.fix: Optimize hot path: check PM2 logs, nginx proxy timeouts, and in-memory ops

checks.label: 250 users: Error rate ≤ 5%
checks.fix: Check backend logs: pm2 logs jarvis-os --lines 50

checks.label: 250 users: CPU < 90%
checks.fix: Optimize CPU-heavy code paths or scale VPS

checks.label: 250 users: Memory < 1.5GB
checks.fix: Investigate memory usage: pm2 monit && heap profiling

checks.label: 500 users: P95 ≤ 5000ms
checks.fix: Optimize hot path: check PM2 logs, nginx proxy timeouts, and in-memory ops

checks.label: 500 users: Error rate ≤ 10%
checks.fix: Check backend logs: pm2 logs jarvis-os --lines 50

checks.label: 500 users: CPU < 90%
checks.fix: Optimize CPU-heavy code paths or scale VPS

checks.label: 500 users: Memory < 1.5GB
checks.fix: Investigate memory usage: pm2 monit && heap profiling

checks.label: Frontend build artifacts present
checks.fix: cd frontend && npm ci && npm run build

checks.label: Backend entry point (backend/server.js)
checks.fix: Ensure backend/server.js exists and is the PM2 entry point

checks.label: PM2 ecosystem config (ecosystem.config.cjs)
checks.fix: PM2 ecosystem config required: pm2 start ecosystem.config.cjs --env production

checks.label: PM2 jarvis-os process online
checks.fix: pm2 start ecosystem.config.cjs --env production && pm2 save

checks.label: Health endpoint /api/health responds 200
checks.fix: Ensure backend is running: pm2 restart jarvis-os && pm2 logs jarvis-os --lines 20

checks.label: Electron auto-updater configured
checks.detail: electron-builder publish config or GH_TOKEN found
checks.fix: Set GH_TOKEN and add publish: {provider: github} to package.json build config

checks.label: PM2 restart capability
checks.fix: pm2 start ecosystem.config.cjs --env production && pm2 save && pm2 startup

checks.label: Git rollback capability (git log reachable)
checks.detail: Last commits: fc5c775 feat(pcs-2): Production Credentials Sprint 2 — External Platform Matrix (8 ecosystems, 39 platforms)
checks.fix: Ensure repo is initialized and commits are present for rollback

checks.label: Backup script present (backup.sh / safe-backup.cjs)
checks.fix: Create backup.sh that tarballs data/ and uploads to S3/R2

checks.label: Backups directory exists
checks.fix: mkdir -p backups && chmod 700 backups

checks.label: Backup exists (< 25h old)
checks.fix: Run: ./backup.sh   OR add to cron: 0 2 * * * cd /opt/jarvis-os && ./backup.sh >> logs/backup.log 2>&1

checks.label: data/ directory has files (flat-file DB)
checks.fix: Ensure data/ is included in backup: tar -czf backup.tar.gz data/

checks.label: .env (config) backup awareness
checks.detail: .env file present — ensure it's backed up securely off-site (NOT in git)
checks.fix: Back up .env to a secrets manager (Doppler, AWS Secrets Manager, Vault) — NEVER commit to git

checks.label: Assets (frontend build / uploads) exist
checks.fix: Include frontend/build and uploads/ in your backup script

checks.label: Restore test script present
checks.fix: Create scripts/test-restore.cjs to validate backup integrity on every backup run

checks.label: Latest backup file is readable
checks.fix: Re-run ./backup.sh and verify tar -tzf backups/latest.tar.gz completes without errors

checks.label: Backup retention ≥ 7 copies
checks.fix: Update backup.sh retention: keep last 14 backups with: ls -t backups/*.tar.gz | tail -n +15 | xargs rm -f

checks.label: Off-site backup configured (S3/R2/rsync)
checks.detail: No S3/R2 credentials — backups are local only
checks.fix: Set AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID + AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY (or R2 keys) and upload: aws s3 cp backups/ s3://your-bucket/ --recursive

checks.label: PM2 process monitoring
checks.fix: pm2 start ecosystem.config.cjs --env production && pm2 monit

checks.label: Memory usage < 90%
checks.fix: Investigate memory leak: pm2 logs jarvis-os --lines 50 && pm2 monit

checks.label: CPU usage < 80%
checks.fix: Scale VPS or optimize hot code paths

checks.label: Disk usage < 85%
checks.fix: Clean old logs: pm2 flush && find logs/ -mtime +7 -delete && df -h /

checks.label: Telegram alerts configured
checks.fix: Set TELEGRAM_TOKEN (from @BotFather) and TELEGRAM_OPERATOR_CHAT_ID in .env

checks.label: Log rotation configured
checks.fix: pm2 install pm2-logrotate && pm2 set pm2-logrotate:max_size 50M && pm2 set pm2-logrotate:retain 7

checks.label: Health endpoint accessible
checks.fix: Ensure the backend is running: pm2 restart jarvis-os

checks.label: Security headers (HSTS, X-Frame, X-Content-Type, CSP, Referrer-Policy)
checks.detail: X-Frame: false, X-Content-Type: false, HSTS: false, CSP: false, Referrer: false
checks.fix: Set security headers in nginx.conf: HSTS, X-Frame-Options DENY, X-Content-Type-Options nosniff, CSP

checks.label: JWT_SECRET ≥ 32 characters
checks.fix: Generate: openssl rand -hex 32  then set JWT_SECRET in .env

checks.label: .env excluded from git (.gitignore)
checks.fix: echo '.env' >> .gitignore && git rm --cached .env 2>/dev/null || true

checks.label: .env permissions 600 (owner read/write only)
checks.fix: chmod 600 .env

checks.label: SSH password authentication disabled
checks.detail: Cannot probe sshd config (may be OK in local dev)
checks.fix: Set PasswordAuthentication no in /etc/ssh/sshd_config then: sudo systemctl reload sshd

checks.label: No unexpected ports open
checks.fix: Close unexpected ports: sudo ufw deny

checks.label: Nginx rate limiting configured
checks.fix: Add limit_req_zone and limit_req directives to nginx.conf (see project nginx.conf for reference)

checks.label: 50 users: P95 ≤ 800ms
checks.fix: Optimize hot path: check PM2 logs, nginx proxy timeouts, and in-memory ops

checks.label: 50 users: Error rate ≤ 1%
checks.fix: Check backend logs: pm2 logs jarvis-os --lines 50

checks.label: 50 users: CPU < 90%
checks.fix: Optimize CPU-heavy code paths or scale VPS

checks.label: 50 users: Memory < 1.5GB
checks.fix: Investigate memory usage: pm2 monit && heap profiling

checks.label: 100 users: P95 ≤ 1500ms
checks.fix: Optimize hot path: check PM2 logs, nginx proxy timeouts, and in-memory ops

checks.label: 100 users: Error rate ≤ 2%
checks.fix: Check backend logs: pm2 logs jarvis-os --lines 50

checks.label: 100 users: CPU < 90%
checks.fix: Optimize CPU-heavy code paths or scale VPS

checks.label: 100 users: Memory < 1.5GB
checks.fix: Investigate memory usage: pm2 monit && heap profiling

checks.label: 250 users: P95 ≤ 3000ms
checks.fix: Optimize hot path: check PM2 logs, nginx proxy timeouts, and in-memory ops

checks.label: 250 users: Error rate ≤ 5%
checks.fix: Check backend logs: pm2 logs jarvis-os --lines 50

checks.label: 250 users: CPU < 90%
checks.fix: Optimize CPU-heavy code paths or scale VPS

checks.label: 250 users: Memory < 1.5GB
checks.fix: Investigate memory usage: pm2 monit && heap profiling

checks.label: 500 users: P95 ≤ 5000ms
checks.fix: Optimize hot path: check PM2 logs, nginx proxy timeouts, and in-memory ops

checks.label: 500 users: Error rate ≤ 10%
checks.fix: Check backend logs: pm2 logs jarvis-os --lines 50

failingChecks.label: Ubuntu 22.04+
failingChecks.fix: Upgrade to Ubuntu 22.04 LTS: do-release-upgrade

failingChecks.label: CPU ≥ 2 cores
failingChecks.fix: Resize VPS to at least 2 vCPUs

failingChecks.label: RAM ≥ 1GB
failingChecks.fix: Resize VPS to at least 2GB RAM

failingChecks.label: Disk ≥ 5GB free
failingChecks.fix: Free disk space or expand volume

failingChecks.label: Swap configured
failingChecks.fix: sudo fallocate -l 2G /swapfile && sudo chmod 600 /swapfile && sudo mkswap /swapfile && sudo swapon /swapfile

failingChecks.label: Timezone (UTC preferred)
failingChecks.fix: sudo timedatectl set-timezone UTC

failingChecks.label: SSH service running
failingChecks.detail: SSH: Command failed: systemctl is-active ssh 2>/dev/null || systemctl is-active sshd 2>/dev/null
failingChecks.fix: sudo systemctl enable --now ssh

failingChecks.label: UFW firewall active
failingChecks.fix: sudo ufw allow 22 && sudo ufw allow 80 && sudo ufw allow 443 && sudo ufw --force enable

failingChecks.label: Fail2Ban active
failingChecks.detail: Fail2Ban: Command failed: systemctl is-active fail2ban 2>/dev/null
failingChecks.fix: sudo apt install fail2ban -y && sudo systemctl enable --now fail2ban

failingChecks.label: Cron daemon active
failingChecks.detail: Cron: Command failed: systemctl is-active cron 2>/dev/null || systemctl is-active crond 2>/dev/null
failingChecks.fix: sudo systemctl enable --now cron

failingChecks.label: PM2 running (≥1 online process)
failingChecks.fix: pm2 start ecosystem.config.cjs --env production

failingChecks.label: Nginx service running
failingChecks.detail: nginx: Command failed: systemctl is-active nginx 2>/dev/null
failingChecks.fix: sudo systemctl start nginx && sudo systemctl enable nginx

failingChecks.label: Nginx config valid (nginx -t)
failingChecks.fix: Edit /etc/nginx/sites-available/ooplix — fix the reported syntax error and sudo nginx -t

failingChecks.label: Ooplix nginx site config exists
failingChecks.fix: sudo cp nginx.conf /etc/nginx/sites-available/ooplix && sudo ln -s /etc/nginx/sites-available/ooplix /etc/nginx/sites-enabled/ && sudo nginx -t && sudo systemctl reload nginx

failingChecks.label: HTTPS (port 443) listening
failingChecks.fix: Ensure SSL cert is installed and nginx is configured for HTTPS

failingChecks.label: HTTP → HTTPS redirect
failingChecks.fix: Add 'return 301 https://$host$request_uri;' in the :80 server block

failingChecks.label: WebSocket proxying configured
failingChecks.detail: WebSocket headers not found in nginx.conf
failingChecks.fix: Add: proxy_http_version 1.1; proxy_set_header Upgrade $http_upgrade; proxy_set_header Connection 'upgrade';

failingChecks.label: SSL certificate file exists
failingChecks.fix: sudo certbot certonly --nginx -d app.ooplix.com -d www.ooplix.com -d ooplix.com

failingChecks.label: Certbot auto-renew active
failingChecks.fix: sudo systemctl enable certbot.timer && sudo systemctl start certbot.timer  OR  add: 0 12 * * * /usr/bin/certbot renew --quiet

failingChecks.label: AAAA record for ooplix.com
failingChecks.fix: Add AAAA record pointing to your VPS IPv6 address
